rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be heard with PTR-14-297800. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator has not filed 4th account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/05/2021. The following issues remain unaddressed since notes were e-mailed to counsel on 11/03/2020: 1) The dollar amount specified in paragraph 6 of the small estate affidavit must be corrected to $166,250. 2) The petitioner's declaration filed on 11/10/2020 does not state that the small estate affidavit was executed in the presence of the petitioner, which is a requirement of reasonable proof of identity under Probate Code section 13104(b)(2) in lieu of a notary public's certificate of acknowledgment.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SERGE JOHN WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/05/2021. 1) Notice of the hearing must be on the most recent version of a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 including a statement in bold face type required by Probate Code section 12201(b). 2) Direct notice must be given to the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate. 3) A copy of the report must be given to Inheritance Funding Company. 4) Notice with a copy of the report must be given to Marc Seidenfeld as no waiver of notice or withdrawal of his request for special notice has been filed. 5) Paragraph 6 of the report alleges that the petitioner's share of the estate is 16.665%, but according to the 08/12/2020 order, the petitioner will not receive any final distribution. 6) Paragraph 7 of the report must include all information required by CRC 7.250(a). 7) Paragraph 12 of the report alleges that all creditor claims have been paid or settled, but no DE-174 form is on file except for the petitioner's rejection of American Express Centurion Bank's claim. 8) No receipts for preliminary distribution have been filed. 9) No election by the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate is on file. Probate Code section 13502. Is the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate contemplating a spousal property petition?

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF BRADLEY A. PAKULA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/12/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No proposed orders have been provided. 2) No contact information form is on file. LRSF 14.78(A)(3). 3) No current status report is on file. LRSF 14.78(J)(3). 4) No original bank statements verifying the ending cash balance are on file. 5) The current bond must be increased before this account can be approved. 6) The beginning estimated market value of the disability benefits is different from the ending value reported in the prior account. 7) The date and amount of each interest payment from Wells Fargo and Ally Bank must be reported on Schedule A. 8) What is the purpose of "GILC - Insurance" reported on Schedule C? 9) Schedule C reports payment of attorney fees before the Court approved the prior account. 10) Did the Court extend the petitioner's authority to pay monthly allowances reported on Schedule C after 06/30/2018? 11) Has the petitioner's attorney prepared an order approving the prior account as directed by the Court's minute order issued on 07/10/2018?

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/2020. 1) No proposed orders have been provided. 2) A supplement addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The decedent was a temporary conservatee. Did she have sufficient testamentary capacity when she created the will? B) The beneficiary named in the will used to be a temporary conservatee. Does he have sufficient capacity to nominate the petitioner and waive a bond? Does he have sufficient capacity to waive a potential conflict of interest based on the petitioner's prior legal representation of the beneficiary in the temporary conservatorship proceeding?
